ocr.text: In the Bechtel Group's FY2023 report on Infrastructure spending allocations, the category breakdown reveals Roads with an
allocation of $50M, a P/E ratio of 1.5, a current ratio of 1.2, liquidity metrics at 200%, leverage ratios of 60%, gross margin of
15%, net profit margin of 8%, and a return on assets of 5%. Bridges received $30M, with a P/E ratio of 1.8, current ratio of 1.1,
liquidity metrics at 150%, leverage ratios of 55%, gross margin of 12%, net profit margin of 7%, and return on assets of 6%.
Railways were allocated $80M, accompanied by a P/E ratio of 2.0, current ratio of 1.4, liquidity metrics at 160%, leverage
ratios of 65%, gross margin of 18%, net profit margin of 9%, and return on assets of 4%. Utilities saw $40M, featuring a P/E
ratio of 1.2, current ratio of 1.0, liquidity metrics at 120%, leverage ratios of 62%, gross margin of 10%, net profit margin of
6%, and return on assets of 3%.

The total allocation sums to $270M, with an overall P/E ratio of 1.6, current ratio of 1.2, liquidity metrics at 150%, leverage
ratios of 59%, gross margin of 13%, net profit margin of 7%, return on assets of 4%, and projected growth of 10%.

The fiscal year is 2023, the region is North America, with the currency in USD, indicating overall trends of increasing

investment, sector growth, and sustainable practices.
